def: That may be calculated or ascertained by calculation.
To cause to shake; to tremble; to move tremulously, as fromweakness; to totter. [Obs.]
Pertaining to, or designating, certain acids analogous to thephosphonic acids, but containing two hydrocarbon radicals, andderived from the secondary phosphines by oxidation.
The state or quality of being dioecious.
Furnished with feathers which conceal the feet.
Of or pertaining to a subscription, or signature. "Thesubscriptive part." Richardson.-- Sub*scrip"tive*ly, adv.
A panel or cushion saddle; the under pad or cushion of saddle;esp., a pad or cushion put on behind a man's saddle, on which a womanmay ride.His [a soldier's] shank pillion without stirrups. Spenser.
Of or pertaining to Hibernia, now Ireland; Irish.-- n.
Capable of being, or proper to be , repaid; due; as, a loanrepayable in ten days; services repayable in kind.
Of or pertaining to the river Styx; hence, hellish; infernal.See Styx.At that so sudden blaze, the Stygian throng Bent their aspect.Milton.
